response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development, including low-latency systems and embedded development, but lacks significant C++ experience in industrial automation or cybersecurity contexts. Key gaps include no demonstrated work with IEC 62443 standards, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA), or Azure cloud services. The resume shows strong expertise in TypeScript, Node.js, and D language, with no direct C++ contributions in relevant domains.
strengths: Proven track record in building scalable, high-performance systems (e.g., real-time metadata handling at Dunnhumby).
Experience with secure development practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and cross-functional collaboration.
Strong problem-solving skills with diverse technical stacks, including low-latency and event-driven architectures.
concerns: No direct C++ experience in industrial automation or cloud-based solutions for industrial settings.
Lacks exposure to IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.).
No evidence of Azure cloud services experience or React/Node.js familiarity as an advantage for cloud integration.

key_factors.factor: C++ and STL experience in industrial automation context
Industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) and secure coding practices
